questions.q: Who gifted the kingdom of Anga to Karna?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana crowned Karna king of Anga on the spot to make him eligible to duel Arjuna and to win his friendship.

questions.q: Where was Karna handed the kingdom of Anga?
options: At the tournament grounds of the Kuru princes
On the banks of the Ganga
questions.explanation: The gift was made in the arena during the princes' graduation tournament, moments after Karna was mocked for his birth.

questions.q: What was the capital city of the kingdom of Anga?
questions.explanation: Anga's capital was Champa, a famous city on the Ganga in the kingdom's heartland.

questions.q: Which of Karna's names means king of Anga?
questions.explanation: Karna became Angaraja, ruler of Anga, while Vasusena, Radheya, and Vaikartana refer to his birth wealth, his foster mother, and the sun.

questions.q: In which direction from the Kuru heartland did Anga lie?
questions.explanation: Anga was an eastern kingdom, far down the Ganga from Hastinapura.

questions.q: What did Duryodhana achieve by gifting Anga in a single gesture?
options: He fulfilled a vow to his father
He made a charioteer's son a king
questions.explanation: The gift instantly raised Karna from a mocked charioteer's son to a crowned king with the rank to challenge princes.

questions.q: What bond did the gift of Anga seal?
options: An alliance between Anga and Gandhara
Peace between the Kurus and Panchalas
Karna's marriage into the Kuru house
questions.explanation: When Karna asked what he could give in return, Duryodhana asked only for friendship, sealing the epic's most fateful loyalty.

questions.q: Before receiving Anga, how was Karna regarded socially?
questions.explanation: Karna was known as the son of the charioteer Adhiratha, a suta's status that barred him from dueling princes.

questions.q: Whose royal sanction validated Duryodhana's gift of Anga?
questions.explanation: Dhritarashtra approved the bestowal, giving his son's theatrical gesture the force of law.

questions.q: On which great river did Anga's capital Champa stand?
questions.explanation: Champa lay on the Ganga, whose lower course flowed through the eastern kingdom of Anga.

questions.q: In the tale told to the Pandavas in the Vana Parva, which sage's arrival ended a long drought in Anga?
questions.explanation: The innocent young ascetic Rishyashringa brought the rains back to Anga the moment he set foot in the kingdom.

questions.q: Which king of Anga brought Rishyashringa to his kingdom?
questions.explanation: King Lomapada, also called Romapada, contrived to bring the sage to drought-stricken Anga.

questions.q: Who was given to Rishyashringa in marriage in Anga?
questions.explanation: Lomapada gave the sage his daughter Shanta in marriage, binding Rishyashringa to Anga.

questions.q: Though king of Anga, where did Karna mostly live?
options: In a camp on Anga's borders
questions.explanation: Karna ruled Anga but spent his days at Hastinapura as Duryodhana's closest friend and counselor.

questions.q: In the Kurukshetra war, on which side did the forces of Anga fight?
questions.explanation: Anga's strength stood with its king Karna and therefore with Duryodhana's Kaurava host.

questions.q: With which modern region is ancient Anga most commonly identified?
questions.explanation: Anga is identified with the country around Bhagalpur in eastern Bihar, where the site of Champa is still pointed out.

questions.q: Which powerful kingdom was Anga's neighbor to the west?
questions.explanation: Anga bordered Magadha, Jarasandha's fortress realm, its great neighbor and frequent rival to the west.

questions.q: For what daily practice did Karna become famous as king of Anga?
options: Boundless giving to anyone who asked
questions.explanation: As Angaraja, Karna was renowned for his charity, especially after his noon worship of the sun, when he refused no petitioner.

questions.q: When Krishna urged Karna to defect before the war, what did Karna's debt for Anga represent?
options: A debt he claimed was long repaid
A trifle beside his true birthright
A bond of loyalty he would not break
A curse he wished to escape
questions.explanation: Karna refused even the Pandava throne, holding that Duryodhana's gift and trust bound him beyond any claim of blood.

questions.q: How does the epic describe the act by which Karna received Anga?
options: An on-the-spot coronation in the arena
A wager won at the dice table
questions.explanation: Karna was consecrated immediately in the tournament arena, anointed by brahmins before the assembled court.

questions.q: What affliction had struck Anga in the days of King Lomapada?
options: A drought when the rains failed
questions.explanation: The rains deserted Anga in Lomapada's reign, and the kingdom withered until Rishyashringa was brought there.

questions.q: What was Champa renowned as in ancient times?
options: A wealthy river port and trading city
questions.explanation: Champa flourished as a rich port on the Ganga, a gateway of trade toward the eastern lands.

questions.q: In the lineage legend, Prince Anga, the kingdom's namesake, was born to which pair?
options: Queen Sudeshna and the sage Dirghatamas
Queen Satyavati and the sage Parashara
Queen Ambika and the sage Vyasa
Queen Sukeshi and the sage Bharadwaja
questions.explanation: The epic tells that the sage Dirghatamas fathered Anga and his brothers on Sudeshna, queen of King Bali, and the kingdoms took their names.

questions.q: Which kingdoms does legend name as founded by the brothers of Prince Anga?
questions.explanation: Anga's brothers Vanga, Kalinga, Pundra, and Suhma gave their names to the neighboring eastern kingdoms.

questions.q: By what earlier name was the capital of Anga known?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Champa was earlier called Malini before taking its later, more famous name.

questions.q: Why had the rains failed in Anga under Lomapada, according to the tale?
options: Indra lost a wager with the king
The king had wronged a brahmin, and Indra withheld the rain
A rakshasa had swallowed the clouds
The royal sacrifice was left unfinished
questions.explanation: Lomapada's offense against a brahmin drove the priests from his land, and Indra withheld rain until the wrong was expiated.

questions.q: How was the innocent ascetic Rishyashringa lured to Anga?
options: By courtesans who charmed him aboard a boat
By a false summons from his father
By the promise of a great sacrifice
By a magical deer he followed
questions.explanation: Courtesans sent by Lomapada beguiled the sheltered young sage, who had never seen a woman, and carried him by boat to Anga.

questions.q: During the conquests before the Rajasuya, which Pandava subdued Anga on his eastern march?
questions.explanation: The Sabha Parva records that Bhima's eastern campaign for Yudhishthira's Rajasuya overcame Karna's kingdom of Anga.

questions.q: Which emperor is said to have later gifted Karna the city of Malini?
questions.explanation: The Shanti Parva relates that Jarasandha, pleased after their duel ended in mutual respect, gave Karna the town of Malini.

questions.q: Who was the father of Rishyashringa, the sage who saved Anga from drought?
questions.explanation: Rishyashringa was the son of the sage Vibhandaka and was born bearing a small horn on his head, which gave him his name.
